records.ety: [Cf. L. necessitatus, p. p. of necessitare, and F. ncessiter. See Necessity.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Necessitated (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Necessitating (?).]

defs: To make necessary or indispensable; to render unavoidable.
quotes: Sickness [might] necessitate his removal from the court.
This fact necessitates a second line.

defs: To reduce to the necessity of; to force; to compel.
quotes: The Marquis of Newcastle, being pressed on both sides, was necessitated to draw all his army into York.
